query: Angular constructor vs ngOnInit vs ngAfterViewInit: when to use

competitors.label: Angular Component Lifecycle
competitors.selectionReason: This is the official lifecycle reference and the strongest canonical baseline for constructor, ngOnInit, and ngAfterViewInit timing.
competitors.winReason: The Angular docs remain authoritative, but our shipped trivia is more directly shaped around the DOM/ViewChild decision and the exact pitfalls interviewers usually ask next.
theirStrengths: It gives the authoritative lifecycle table, execution order, and the official wording for when each hook runs.
theirGaps: It is not organized as a direct decision guide for DOM/ViewChild access versus constructor or ngOnInit.

realWorldUseCases: Use ngAfterViewInit() for DOM/ViewChild access in most cases.
followUpConfusion: @ViewChild(..., { static: true }) resolves earlier (before/at init), so it can be used in ngOnInit() if the element is always present
If you change a bound value inside ngAfterViewInit , you can trigger ExpressionChangedAfterItHasBeenCheckedError in dev mode.

realWorldUseCases: The official guide explains each lifecycle phase and mentions that ngAfterViewInit is where view queries can be read, but it is less question-shaped around the DOM/ViewChild choice itself.
actionableExamples: The docs include a lifecycle summary table plus concrete hook examples, so the example quality stays competitive with ours.
followUpConfusion: Angular.dev covers view-query timing and ExpressionChangedAfterItHasBeenCheckedError, but it does not combine constructor, ngOnInit, static queries, and DOM access into one interview-style answer.

actionableExamples: Add one broken-before-fixed-after example showing a ViewChild under *ngIf so the applied example edge stays clearer than the docs.

competitors.label: GreatFrontEnd Angular Basic Interview Questions
competitors.selectionReason: GreatFrontEnd has a directly relevant Angular basics guide that includes lifecycle hooks, so it is the strongest interview-prep calibration point for this fundamentals topic.
competitors.winReason: GreatFrontEnd covers the hook names well, but our page is much more specific about DOM/ViewChild access, static queries, and the expression-changed pitfall.
theirStrengths: It maps the common lifecycle hooks in a concise interview-friendly format and includes quick code examples.
theirGaps: It is lighter on the DOM/ViewChild decision tree and on the static:true nuance that usually trips candidates.

realWorldUseCases: Use ngAfterViewInit() for DOM/ViewChild access in most cases.
followUpConfusion: @ViewChild(..., { static: true }) resolves earlier (before/at init), so it can be used in ngOnInit() if the element is always present
If you change a bound value inside ngAfterViewInit , you can trigger ExpressionChangedAfterItHasBeenCheckedError in dev mode.

realWorldUseCases: GreatFrontEnd names ngOnInit and ngAfterViewInit, but it stays at the lifecycle-hooks overview level rather than centering the DOM/ViewChild use case.
actionableExamples: Its examples are concise and interview-ready enough that the example comparison remains close.
followUpConfusion: The guide does not go as far into static ViewChild timing or the common expression-changed follow-up.

actionableExamples: Add one tiny constructor-vs-ngOnInit-vs-ngAfterViewInit anti-example so the practical delta is visible even faster.

competitors.label: Angular University ViewChild Guide
competitors.selectionReason: This is a strong tutorial competitor because it goes deep on ViewChild timing and AfterViewInit behavior, which are the core follow-ups for this trivia.
competitors.winReason: Angular University is still deeper on ViewChild as a tutorial, but our page now makes the wrong static:true fix and the afterNextRender follow-up much more explicit in one lifecycle-decision answer.
theirStrengths: It goes deep on ViewChild timing and shows several scenarios beyond the bare lifecycle definitions.
theirGaps: It is more ViewChild-centric than constructor-versus-ngOnInit-versus-ngAfterViewInit comparative.

realWorldUseCases: Static queries are not “faster DOM access.” They only work early when the queried node is guaranteed to be present in the initial template.
followUpConfusion: newer Angular also gives you render callbacks like afterNextRender.

realWorldUseCases: Angular University is also built around the real ViewChild timing question, but our page now highlights the broken static:true-under-*ngIf case more directly.
actionableExamples: Its walkthrough is still broader and more example-heavy than our single-page trivia answer, so the example criterion stays close.
followUpConfusion: It addresses timing and AfterViewInit well, but our page now adds the afterNextRender follow-up and the specific static-query failure mode.

actionableExamples: Add one second code example that contrasts default ViewChild timing with static:true timing more explicitly.
